claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have a child I'm asking about - my son Alex. He's 21 now and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see that Alex is 21 years old.

To determine eligibility, I need to ask: Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

This is important because Child Benefit normally stops when a child turns 16, unless they continue in approved education or training - in which case it can continue until they turn 20. Since Alex is 21, this is a key factor.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished his studies a while back.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to make a determination about Alex's eligibility.

Let me assess this carefully:

- Alex is 21 years old
- He is not currently in approved education or training
- He finished his studies some time ago

According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are under 20 and stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 21 years old and not in approved education or training, he is above the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**I'm sorry, but you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Unfortunately, Alex does not qualify because he is 21 years old. Child Benefit is available for:
- Children under 16, or
- Young people aged 16-19 (under 20) who are in approved education or training

Since Alex is 21 and not in education or training, he is above the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Child Benefit would have stopped when Alex either turned 20 or left education/training, whichever came first.

If you have any other questions or if there are any other children you'd like to ask about, please let me know.

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility normally applies to children under 16. For children aged 16 or over, Child Benefit can continue only if the child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit of 20 for children in education or training, and therefore does not qualify for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Alex is above the age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility, which requires children to be either under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility normally applies to children under 16. For children aged 16 or over, Child Benefit can continue only if the child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit of 20 for children in education or training, and therefore does not qualify for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Alex is above the age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility, which requires children to be either under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
